UX Audit

InstaOffer / UX Audit Report

instaoffer.vercel.app FigJam Wireframe May 2026
3
Critical
5
High
6
Medium
4
Low
5
Passes
58
Overall UX Score
Needs Work
Navigation
Overview
User Flows 2
Homepage 5
Valuation Flow 4
Navigation & IA 3
Dealer Experience 3
Heuristics
Fix Roadmap
🎯

Audit Overview

Scope: live site vs FigJam wireframe flow — seller & dealer journeys

What Works

  • ✓ Clear value prop headline
  • ✓ 5-step process visible on homepage
  • ✓ Trust signals present (privacy, non-binding)
  • ✓ Dealer alert card example is compelling
  • ✓ Social proof numbers (500+ cars, 30+ dealers)

Top Issues

  • ● Valuation page shows only "Loading…"
  • ● /urgent-sale renders empty shell
  • ● No fallback / error states anywhere
  • ● 4 seller paths with no clear primary CTA
  • ● Nav bar has conflicting dual CTAs

Wireframe Gaps

  • ● Offer comparison screen not built
  • ● Phone approval flow missing
  • ● Trade-in journey stub only
  • ● Buy request flow stub only
  • ● Dealer dashboard behind login wall
Methodology
How This Audit Was Conducted
This audit covers: (1) Live site crawl of instaoffer.vercel.app — homepage, /valuation, /urgent-sale, /for-dealers pages; (2) FigJam wireframe flow analysis (QqTc15FAXJg2eV4LWFFEx3); (3) Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics scoring; (4) Conversion-focused UX review against a Qatar used-car market user model. Severity: Critical = conversion blocker / broken, High = significant friction, Medium = UX debt, Low = polish.
🔄

User Flow Analysis

Wireframe intent vs. live implementation gaps

52
/ 100

The FigJam wireframe defines 4 seller journeys + 1 dealer journey. Here's the current delivery status:

Journey Wireframe Status Live Status Gap
Know My Car Value
→ /valuation		 ✅ Fully wireframed (multi-step form → estimate → offer request) ⚠ Renders "Loading…" only — JS-dependent, broken without auth/data Critical
Urgent Sale
→ /urgent-sale		 ✅ Wireframed — urgency flag, expedited offer timeline ⚠ Page renders empty shell (just header + footer) Critical
Trade-In
→ /trade-in		 🔶 Partially wireframed ⚠ Route exists but content unclear from crawl High
Find Next Car
→ /buy-request		 🔶 Partially wireframed ⚠ Route exists but likely stub High
Dealer Flow
→ /for-dealers → /dashboard		 ✅ Wireframed — alert feed, bid flow Dealer landing exists; dashboard behind login (OK for now) Medium
Critical
Core conversion flow is broken in production
The primary user action — "Get My Free Valuation" — leads to a page that shows only "Loading…" in production. This is likely a client-side rendering issue where the React/Next.js app fails to hydrate without a backend connection. Any real user clicking the main CTA sees nothing. This kills conversion entirely.
Fix → Add a static fallback UI for the valuation form that renders server-side. At minimum, show the form fields even if the AI estimate isn't ready. Add a loading skeleton, not a blank screen.
Next.js SSRHydrationP0
High
4 parallel entry points with no clear primary path
The homepage presents four equally-weighted seller journeys. Wireframe intent (from FigJam) suggests these should be sequential or tiered. The "Popular" badge on "Sell my car fast" is too subtle — it fails to create a clear primary CTA. Users in Qatar's market (often first-time platform users) will experience decision paralysis.
Fix → Make "Sell my car fast" the dominant primary CTA with a full-width button treatment. Collapse the other 3 options into a secondary "or choose another path" expandable row. Reduce cognitive load at entry.
HomepageCTA HierarchyDecision Paralysis
🏠

Homepage UX

instaoffer.vercel.app — above fold, content sections, CTAs

64
/ 100
Pass
Hero headline is clear and benefit-focused
"What do you want to do today?" + 4 goal options is a solid intent-based navigation pattern. The 🇶🇦 localization signal ("Designed for Qatar's car market") is a strong trust anchor for the target audience.
High
Dual CTAs in nav bar create conflict
The navbar contains both "Sell My Car" (text link) and "Sell My Car" (button) — two identical-label CTAs going to the same /valuation route. This is redundant noise. Additionally "New Cars" in the navbar is disconnected from the seller-focused value proposition of the entire page — it's an unfulfilled promise (likely a stub).
Fix → Remove the text-link "Sell My Car" from nav. Keep only the button. Remove "New Cars" link until the /cars page is built with real inventory. Replace with "How It Works" as a trust-building nav item.
NavbarCTA Redundancy
Medium
Stats section feels unverified — no source or timeframe
"500+ Cars Sold", "30+ Verified Dealers", "< 5 min to get estimate" — no timeframe, no source, no third-party badge. For a new platform in Qatar, these numbers alone don't build credibility without context. Qatar users are skeptical of new platforms.
Fix → Add "since launch" or "this quarter" framing. Add a dealer logo strip or a testimonial from even one real user. Consider linking "30+ Verified Dealers" to a /dealers directory page.
Social ProofTrust
Medium
Car make grid lacks context — why is this here?
The "Value Any Car Make" section with brand buttons is visually busy and its purpose is unclear to a first-time user. Clicking "Toyota" takes you to /valuation?make=Toyota but there's no explanation of what that pre-selection does. The section appears to be SEO-driven but adds friction to the UI.
Fix → Add a microcopy intro: "Start with your car's make to get a faster estimate." Reduce to the top 6 makes by Qatar market share. Consider showing approximate price ranges per make to make this section feel more valuable.
Content ClaritySEO vs UX
Medium
Footer has 3 sections but some links are dead
"My Offers" in footer links to /my-offers with no indication it requires login. "How It Works" in footer goes to /how-it-works but nav bar's "Sell My Car" doesn't include a How It Works link. The 5-step homepage section IS how it works — inconsistent information architecture.
Fix → Add "(requires sign in)" label to /my-offers link. Consolidate the 5-step section into a dedicated /how-it-works page and link from nav. Audit all footer links for 404s before launch.
IAFooterDead Links
Low
Arabic language option is missing
The site claims to be built for Qatar's market but is English-only. A significant portion of Qatar's used car sellers (particularly older residents and those outside the expat bubble) communicate primarily in Arabic.
Fix → Add a language toggle (EN / عربي) in the navbar. Even a partial Arabic translation of the valuation form would significantly expand the addressable audience.
LocalizationAccessibilityArabic
💡

Valuation Flow

/valuation — the core conversion funnel

30
/ 100
Critical
Page renders "Loading…" — complete conversion blocker
The /valuation route returns only a "Loading…" text with no form, no content, and no error state. The page appears to depend entirely on client-side JavaScript with no SSR or static fallback. In production, this means 100% of users who click the primary CTA see a blank loading state — and likely bounce.
Fix → Implement the form as a statically-renderable multi-step component. Use progressive enhancement: render the first step (Make/Model/Year) as pure HTML, then layer in the ML estimate API call. Add a ProgressBar component matching the wireframe's step indicator.
P0 BlockerSSRCritical Path
Critical
Wireframe's step-by-step form doesn't exist in implementation
The FigJam wireframe shows a clear multi-step valuation form: (1) Make/Model, (2) Year/Mileage, (3) Condition/Notes, (4) Estimate reveal, (5) Request offers. The live site has none of these steps rendered. The wireframe's progress indicator, step labels, and field validation states are all absent.
Fix → Build the form as a controlled multi-step wizard with URL-based step state (/valuation?step=2) so users can share/bookmark progress. Match the wireframe's field sequence exactly — it was designed for minimal cognitive load.
Wireframe GapForm UXMulti-step
High
No phone number privacy reassurance at the moment of data collection
The homepage prominently says "Phone number stays private" — but this reassurance must be repeated at the exact moment the valuation form asks for a phone number. Trust signals need to be contextual, not just on the homepage. The wireframe appears to show this inline but it's not implemented.
Fix → Add inline microcopy next to the phone field: "🔒 Only shared if you approve a dealer contact." Add a tooltip icon explaining the privacy model. This is a conversion-critical moment.
TrustPrivacyInline Copy
Medium
No error handling or offline state designed
If the ML estimate API fails or times out, there is no fallback experience visible in either the live site or (from wireframe) an error state. For Qatar's network conditions (and Vercel cold starts), a slow estimate is very plausible.
Fix → Design an "estimate unavailable" fallback that still lets users request dealer offers with a manual price input. Add a 5-second timeout with a "Taking longer than usual — we'll email you" fallback message.
Error StatesResilienceFallback UX
🗺️

Navigation & Information Architecture

Wayfinding, menu structure, and content hierarchy

61
/ 100
High
"For Dealers" nav item has no dropdown or landing clarity
The navbar "For Dealers" link navigates to /for-dealers but presents itself ambiguously — it could be a login, a pricing page, or an info page. Dealers coming from search or referral won't know what they're getting. The wireframe suggests a distinct dealer-facing nav state.
Fix → Split the nav into two clear audiences with a toggle or sub-nav: "Sell My Car" and "I'm a Dealer." Alternatively, create a dedicated dealer landing page with a clear hero and distinct visual identity.
IAAudience SegmentationNav
Medium
No breadcrumbs or back navigation in sub-flows
Multi-step flows (valuation, urgent sale) have no visible way to go back to a previous step or to the homepage without using browser back. The wireframe shows a step indicator but no explicit back button. Mobile users especially will be disoriented.
Fix → Add "← Back" text link at top of each step. Implement browser history correctly so the native back button works per step. Add a step progress indicator (Step 2 of 5) at top of each form screen.
BreadcrumbsMulti-step Navigation
Medium
"My Offers" is in footer but has no authenticated session state
A logged-out user clicking "My Offers" is redirected to a login page (or a broken state). There's no unauthenticated landing that explains what offers are, how to get them, or how to sign up. This is a dead-end for curious users.
Fix → Create an unauthenticated /my-offers page with a teaser state: "You don't have any offers yet. Get your first valuation →" This converts a dead end into a funnel entry point.
Auth StatesEmpty StatesFunnel
🏢

Dealer Experience

/for-dealers — acquisition, pricing, dashboard

68
/ 100
Pass
Dealer alert card example is strong and specific
The example alert card on the homepage (2022 Toyota Land Cruiser, Score 87, 295k vs 325k market, ~30k margin) is exactly the right level of specificity. It answers the dealer's core question: "Is this a good deal?" before they even sign up. This is excellent conversion design for the dealer audience.
High
1,000 QAR/month pricing is stated once without a clear plan structure
The dealer section mentions "Only 1,000 QAR / month" but doesn't show what's included — no feature list, no comparison to alternatives, no free trial offer. Dealers won't pay without understanding what they get. This is a conversion drop-off in the dealer funnel.
Fix → Create a clear pricing table with 3 tiers or a feature checklist below the price. Add "First 30 days free" or "Pay after first deal" as a risk-reduction hook. Link to /for-dealers for the full breakdown.
Pricing UXDealer FunnelConversion
Medium
Dealer dashboard linked but requires login with no signup path visible
The footer links to /dashboard and /login?role=dealer, but the homepage dealer section has no visible "Create Dealer Account" CTA. Dealers who want to sign up must know to click "Dealer Login" and then find a registration option there.
Fix → Add a prominent "Apply as a Dealer" or "Start Free Trial" button directly in the dealer section on the homepage. Dealer registration should be a distinct, prominent path — not buried in a login flow.
OnboardingDealer RegistrationCTA
📐

Nielsen's 10 Heuristics

Systematic usability evaluation against industry-standard criteria

# Heuristic Score Key Issue
1 Visibility of System Status 2/10 "Loading…" with no spinner, no progress, no timeout state. Users have zero feedback that anything is happening.
2 Match Between System & Real World 8/10 Language is clear and colloquial ("Sell My Car", "Get My Free Valuation"). "AI-powered" may need explanation for less tech-savvy Qatar users.
3 User Control & Freedom 3/10 No visible back navigation in flows. No way to cancel or restart a valuation. No undo for offer requests.
4 Consistency & Standards 6/10 Dual "Sell My Car" CTAs in nav. Inconsistent link treatment between nav and footer. "How It Works" exists as section but also as a footer link to a separate page.
5 Error Prevention 2/10 No form validation visible. No confirmation for destructive actions. No guard against submitting incomplete data.
6 Recognition Rather Than Recall 7/10 Icon-based step labels help. Car make grid is good recognition pattern. Dealer alert card uses familiar auction/listing format.
7 Flexibility & Efficiency of Use 4/10 No keyboard shortcuts. No "quick estimate" shortcut for repeat users. Car make buttons are good accelerators but lack any power-user features.
8 Aesthetic & Minimalist Design 6/10 Homepage is slightly over-sectioned — 7 distinct content sections creates scroll fatigue. Car make grid and dealer section could be combined or condensed.
9 Help Users Recognize & Recover from Errors 1/10 No error messages anywhere visible. Empty pages (/urgent-sale) provide zero guidance. "Loading…" doesn't tell the user what went wrong or what to do next.
10 Help & Documentation 5/10 The 5-step explainer is on the homepage. No FAQ, no tooltip help, no chat support for users confused during the valuation flow.
🗓️

Fix Roadmap

Prioritized action plan to move UX score from 58 → 80+

1
Critical Fixes — Ship This Week
Days 1–5
🔴
Fix /valuation SSR — render form on server, not just client The page must show Step 1 (Make/Model) on first load, even before JS hydrates. This is the #1 conversion blocker.
🔴
Build /urgent-sale page content Currently an empty shell. Add the urgency flow from the wireframe: flag input, expedited timeline, matching logic.
🔴
Add loading skeleton and timeout fallback to all async routes Replace "Loading…" text with a skeleton UI + 5s timeout with a "something went wrong" message + support link.
🔴
Remove dead/stub nav links ("New Cars") Any nav link to an unbuilt page destroys trust. Remove or replace with coming-soon modal.
2
High Impact — Sprint 2
Days 6–14
🟠
Implement multi-step valuation form matching wireframe 5 steps, step indicator, back navigation, URL-based step state, inline validation, privacy microcopy on phone field.
🟠
Consolidate homepage CTAs — make primary path dominant Elevate "Sell my car fast" to a full-width hero CTA. Collapse other 3 options into secondary "or choose another path" row.
🟠
Add "Apply as Dealer" CTA on homepage dealer section Dealers need a visible registration path. Add a prominent "Start Free Trial →" button with a 30-day free offer.
🟠
Build dealer pricing table on /for-dealers Feature list, QAR pricing, comparison to Mzad/OLX alternatives, testimonial from one pilot dealer.
3
UX Polish — Sprint 3
Days 15–30
🟡
Add Arabic language toggle (EN / عربي) At minimum translate: homepage hero, valuation form labels, trust badges. Full translation in Q2.
🟡
Improve social proof — add dealer logos, testimonials, verified timestamps Replace bare stats with contextualized proof: "32 verified dealers including [logos]" and real seller testimonials.
🟡
Build /my-offers unauthenticated empty state Convert the dead-end into a funnel entry. Show a teaser of what offers look like + "Get Your First Valuation →".
🟡
Add WhatsApp as a contact option throughout the funnel Qatar market heavily uses WhatsApp. Add WA contact option at offer stage — sellers trust it more than email forms.
